tags.name: Legal Holds
tags.description: Operations for setting, listing, retrieving, and releasing legal holds. An active hold blocks all deletion and purge of the evidence it covers until the hold is released. Every hold lifecycle event is audited.

tags.name: Policy Versions
tags.description: Operations for browsing policy version history, diffing revisions, and rolling back to a prior revision.

tags.name: Documents
tags.description: Operations for retrieving asynchronously redacted documents.

tags.name: Custom Lists
tags.description: Operations for creating and managing custom lists.

tags.name: Always/Never Redact Lists
tags.description: Operations for managing an account's always-redact and never-redact term lists.

tags.name: Re-identification
tags.description: Governed, audited reversal of cryptographically-redacted values.

tags.name: Policies
tags.description: Operations for creating, retrieving, deleting, and compiling redaction policies.

tags.name: Explain
tags.description: Redact text and return a detailed explanation of what was redacted and why.

tags.name: Redaction Ledger
tags.description: Operations for viewing, exporting, verifying, and deleting redaction-ledger chains.

tags.name: Contexts
tags.description: Operations for creating and managing contexts.

tags.name: Status
tags.description: Unauthenticated health and status, including the supported redaction policy schema version.

tags.name: Signing
tags.description: Retrieve the public signing key used to verify Philter output signatures.

get.summary: Get the always-redact and never-redact lists.
get.description: Returns the account's always-redact and never-redact lists. Both lists are always present; an account with no saved terms returns empty arrays. Admins may read another user's lists by passing that user's email as owner.

put.summary: Append to the always-redact and never-redact lists.
put.description: Appends the supplied terms to the existing lists rather than replacing them - use POST to replace. Each field's terms are added to the current contents of that list; a list that is omitted or sent as an empty array is left unchanged. Terms are trimmed, blank entries are dropped, and terms already present are not added again. The resulting list may contain up to 1000 terms, and each term may be up to 100 characters; an append that would exceed the limit is rejected. Admins may append to another user's lists by passing that user's email as owner.

400.description: The request body is malformed, the resulting list has too many terms, or a term is too long.

post.summary: Replace the always-redact and never-redact lists.
post.description: Replaces both lists in full with the supplied contents - this is not a merge. Each field is the complete desired contents of that list; a list that is omitted or sent as an empty array is cleared. Terms are trimmed and blank entries are dropped. Each list may contain up to 1000 terms, and each term may be up to 100 characters. Admins may replace another user's lists by passing that user's email as owner.

400.description: The request body is malformed, a list has too many terms, or a term is too long.

get.summary: Get the details of a context.
get.description: Get the details of a context with the provided name.

404.description: A context with the given name does not exist.

put.description: Update the entity_type_disambiguation and ledger flags on an existing context.

400.description: The context could not be deleted.

409.description: The context has open asynchronous redaction jobs and cannot be deleted.

post.description: Reverses one or more values that were redacted via the CRYPTO_REPLACE or FPE_ENCRYPT_REPLACE strategy. The caller must supply a stated reason, which is recorded in the audit log alongside the actor, timestamp, and count of reversals. A user may re-identify their own values; an admin may re-identify any user's values by supplying that user's email via the owner parameter. CRYPTO_REPLACE requires policyName so the key can be resolved from the stored policy. FPE_ENCRYPT_REPLACE uses the user's per-account FPE key; if the policy specified a custom FPE key, also pass policyName so that key is used instead.

200.description: Request processed; inspect each result for per-value errors.

404.description: Named policy not found, or owner not found.

get.summary: Get the names of existing policies.
get.description: Returns the names of the caller's policies, paged. Admins may list another user's policies by passing that user's email as owner.

post.description: Saves the policy supplied in the request body under the given name, overwriting any existing policy of the same name. The policy is validated before it is stored. Admins may save into another user's account by passing that user's email as owner.

400.description: The policy name is missing or the policy is invalid.

401.description: The Authorization header is absent or the API key is not recognized.

201.description: The policy was saved and is now active. A policy_activated audit event is recorded.

404.description: The owner does not exist, or the caller is not an admin.

post.summary: Roll back a policy to a prior revision.
post.description: Restores the content of the specified revision as a new head revision, preserving the full history. The live policy is updated with the prior content and its revision counter is incremented. The rollback is audited. Admins may roll back another user's policy via the owner parameter.

400.description: The policy name or target revision is missing.

401.description: The Authorization header is absent or the API key is not recognized.

201.description: Rollback succeeded. Body contains the new revision number.

404.description: The policy or the target revision does not exist.

409.description: Managed policies cannot be rolled back.

post.description: Compiles a policy authored in PhiSQL into the native Phileas policy format. The request body is PhiSQL source; the response carries the policy name and description and the compiled policy JSON, which can then be saved via POST /api/policies.

400.description: The PhiSQL failed to parse/compile, or the compiled policy failed validation.

post.description: Create a list whose contents are the request body, or overwrite it if a list with the same name already exists.

400.description: The list name is empty, the list contains too many items (maximum 100), or an item is too long (maximum 50 characters).

200.description: An existing list with the same name was updated.

412.description: The maximum number of lists already exists.

delete.description: Delete a list with the given name.

404.description: The given list does not exist.

get.summary: List legal holds.
get.description: Returns the caller's active legal holds, paged and ordered by set date descending. Admins may list another user's holds via the owner parameter.

401.description: The Authorization header is absent or the API key is not recognized.

200.description: Array of legal holds, most recently set first.

404.description: The owner does not exist, or the caller is not an admin.

post.description: Creates a named hold that blocks deletion and purge of the specified evidence until the hold is released. The reference must be unique for the calling user. Two scope types are supported: 'document_chain' protects a specific document's ledger chain; 'user' protects all governance evidence owned by the user. Admins may place a hold on another user's evidence via the owner parameter.

400.description: Required fields are missing or the scope type is invalid.

401.description: The Authorization header is absent or the API key is not recognized.

201.description: The hold was set and is now active.

404.description: The owner does not exist, or the caller is not an admin.

409.description: A hold with the given reference already exists for this user.

post.summary: Filter a PDF and receive a redacted PDF in return.
post.description: Asynchronous by default. Returns 202 Accepted with `{"documentId":...}` and a `Location: /api/documents/{documentId}` header. Append `?async=false` to receive the redacted PDF bytes inline (200 OK).

500.description: Signing is enabled but the signing operation failed.

202.description: Job accepted; poll /api/documents/{documentId}/status and download from /api/documents/{documentId}.

post.summary: Redact text and explain the redactions.
post.description: Redacts the plain-text request body using the given policy and context, returning the filtered text together with an explanation of every span that was redacted (type, position, and applied filter). Use the c query parameter to select a context and p to select a policy. When output signing is enabled in Admin Settings, the response includes an `X-Philter-Signature` header containing an ES256 JWT that binds the SHA-256 hash of the response body, the applied policy name and version, a per-response UUID, and an issue timestamp. Verify the signature using the public key from `GET /api/signing-key`.

500.description: Signing is enabled but the signing operation failed.

200.description: Filtered text with explanation. Includes `X-Philter-Signature` JWT header when output signing is enabled.

get.summary: Get the names of existing contexts.
get.description: Get the names of existing contexts.

400.description: The context could not be created.

409.description: A context with this name already exists.

post.summary: Import a mapping table into a context.
post.description: Imports token-to-replacement mappings (as produced by the export endpoint) into a context. By default an incoming token that already exists in the context is skipped; pass on_conflict=overwrite to replace existing replacements instead.

400.description: The payload or on_conflict value is invalid.

get.summary: Get the public signing key.
get.description: Returns the operator's ES256 (ECDSA P-256) public signing key in PEM, JWK, and fingerprint form. Use the public key to verify the `X-Philter-Signature` JWT header returned on successful `POST /api/filter` and `POST /api/explain` responses when output signing is enabled. This endpoint does not require authentication.

get.summary: Get a policy.
get.description: Returns the full policy with the given name. Admins may retrieve another user's policy by passing that user's email as owner.

404.description: A policy with the given name does not exist.

delete.description: Deletes the policy with the given name. Admins may delete another user's policy by passing that user's email as owner.

get.summary: List retained versions of a policy.
get.description: Returns a summary of each retained snapshot for the named policy, ordered by revision descending (most recent first). Each entry carries the revision number, the timestamp the snapshot was captured, and its content hash. Admins may browse another user's history via the owner parameter.

401.description: The Authorization header is absent or the API key is not recognized.

200.description: Array of version summaries, most recent first.

404.description: The named policy does not exist, or a non-admin caller named another user as owner.

get.summary: Fetch a specific revision of a policy.
get.description: Returns the full policy JSON as it existed at the given revision. The response shape is identical to GET /api/policies/{policyName}. Admins may fetch another user's revision via the owner parameter.

401.description: The Authorization header is absent or the API key is not recognized.

200.description: Full policy JSON at the requested revision.

404.description: The policy or the requested revision does not exist.

get.summary: Diff two revisions of a policy.
get.description: Returns an RFC 6902 JSON Patch array describing what changed between the 'from' revision and the 'to' revision. If from and to are omitted, the two most recent retained revisions are compared. Object-level changes (adds, removes, replaces) are reported per field path; array values that differ are reported as a single replace at the array path. Admins may diff another user's policy via owner.

400.description: The policy name is missing, fewer than two retained revisions exist for a default diff, or only one of from/to was supplied.

401.description: The Authorization header is absent or the API key is not recognized.

200.description: Diff envelope containing the compared revision numbers and an RFC 6902 changes array.

404.description: The policy or a requested revision does not exist.

get.summary: Get the names of existing lists.
get.description: Get the names of existing lists.

get.summary: Get the contents of a list.
get.description: Get the contents of a list with the provided name.

409.description: A list with the given does not exist.

get.summary: List redaction-ledger chains.
get.description: Returns the head (genesis entry) of each redacted document's ledger chain, most recent first. Pass q to filter by document id or filename. Admins may list another user's chains by passing that user's email as owner.

get.summary: Get a document's ledger chain.
get.description: Returns the full ordered chain of ledger entries for a document, along with whether the hash chain currently verifies.

get.summary: Verify a document's ledger chain.
get.description: Returns whether the hash chain for the document's ledger verifies (no entry has been altered and every link is intact).

get.summary: Export a document's ledger chain.
get.description: Returns the full ledger chain for a document as a portable JSON document that can be archived and later re-verified. The export contains the decrypted token and replacement values, so treat it as sensitive.

get.summary: Get a legal hold.
get.description: Returns the hold with the given reference. Admins may retrieve another user's hold via the owner parameter.

401.description: The Authorization header is absent or the API key is not recognized.

404.description: No hold with the given reference exists for this user.

delete.description: Removes the hold with the given reference. Once released, evidence previously covered by this hold may become eligible for deletion or purge if no other holds remain. Releasing a hold is audited. Admins may release another user's hold via the owner parameter.

401.description: The Authorization header is absent or the API key is not recognized.

404.description: No hold with the given reference exists for this user.

get.summary: Get the status of Philter, including the supported redaction policy schema version.
get.description: Unauthenticated endpoint returning health, the application version, the supported redaction policy schema version, and the build's git commit. Both /api/status and /api/health return the same response.

get.summary: Get the status of Philter, including the supported redaction policy schema version.
get.description: Unauthenticated endpoint returning health, the application version, the supported redaction policy schema version, and the build's git commit. Both /api/status and /api/health return the same response.

get.summary: List documents submitted for async redaction.
Download the redacted document.
Get the status of an async redaction.
List entries within a context.

delete.summary: Empty all entries from a context.

get.summary: Export a context's mapping table.
get.description: Returns the complete token-to-replacement mapping table for a context in a portable JSON form that can be re-imported into another context or environment. Only token hashes (never the original tokens) and their replacements are returned.

delete.summary: Delete a single context entry by id.
